description: Reject reference-identity projection bindings that resolve to zero logical identity fields. Acceptance: read-plan compilation must fail deterministically when a `DocumentReferenceBinding` has no `IdentityBindings` or when grouped logical-field resolution yields an empty field set; the failure message must identify the offending reference object path/table; valid reference bindings must continue to compile unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a unit test that mutates the existing projection fixture so `DocumentReferenceBinding.IdentityBindings = []` and assert `ReadPlanCompiler.Compile(...)` throws the expected deterministic exception.
Add a normalization/round-trip regression test, if needed, so an empty-field reference projection cannot survive decode/lookup validation.

description: Validate that `DocumentReferenceBinding.TargetResource` matches the owning FK column's `DbColumnModel.TargetResource` before emitting `ReferenceIdentityProjectionBinding` metadata. Acceptance: a target-resource mismatch must fail deterministically during read-plan compilation and during decoded-plan validation, with the error naming the reference object path, table, compiled target resource, and expected target resource; matching bindings must still compile and decode successfully.
steps-to-verify: Add a unit test that mutates the projection fixture so the document-reference binding targets a different resource than the FK column and assert `ReadPlanCompiler.Compile(...)` throws the expected deterministic exception.
Add a regression test that feeds the same mismatch through normalized plan decode or mapping-set lookup validation and assert it is rejected there as well.

description: Validate that each `DescriptorEdgeSource.DescriptorResource` matches the descriptor FK column's `DbColumnModel.TargetResource`, including key-unified/storage-resolved descriptor sources. Acceptance: a descriptor-resource mismatch must fail deterministically during descriptor projection compilation and during decoded-plan validation; the failure must identify the descriptor value path, table, compiled descriptor resource, and expected descriptor resource; valid descriptor sources, including deduplicated key-unified cases, must continue to compile unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a unit test that mutates the projection fixture so a descriptor edge source advertises a different descriptor resource than its FK column and assert `ReadPlanCompiler.Compile(...)` throws the expected deterministic exception.

Add a regression test that covers normalized decode or mapping-set lookup validation for the same mismatch, plus a key-unified descriptor case to confirm storage-column resolution still validates the correct target resource.

description: Enforce that every key-unified reference-identity alias uses the owning `DocumentReferenceBinding.FkColumn` as its presence gate, including single-member logical-field groups. Acceptance: read-plan compilation must fail deterministically when a grouped reference field resolves through `ColumnStorage.UnifiedAlias` with any other `PresenceColumn`; the failure must identify the reference object path, grouped logical field path, resolved presence column, and expected FK column; valid single-member and multi-member key-unified reference projections must continue to compile unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a unit test that mutates the existing key-unified reference projection fixture so a single-member logical field points at a `UnifiedAlias` whose `PresenceColumn` is not the owning reference FK, and assert `ReadPlanCompiler.Compile(...)` throws the expected deterministic exception.
Keep the existing grouped multi-member presence-gate tests green to confirm the stricter check does not regress valid grouped-reference behavior.

description: Reject descriptor projection plans with a null `ResultShape` during normalized read-plan decode instead of allowing a null dereference. Acceptance: `NormalizedPlanContractCodec.Decode(...)` must throw a deterministic argument/validation exception that names `DescriptorProjectionPlanDto.ResultShape` when the DTO omits it; the decoder must still accept valid descriptor projection plans and preserve the existing projection-contract validation messages for non-null but invalid ordinal values.
steps-to-verify: Add a unit test that mutates an encoded read plan so one `DescriptorProjectionPlanDto.ResultShape` is null and assert decode fails with a deterministic exception that identifies the missing `ResultShape` field.
Re-run the existing descriptor projection decode tests to confirm valid plans still round-trip and ordinal-shape validation behavior is unchanged.
